description: Legal document. Deed of dedication of a house in Damascus to the Great Synagogue, ca. 1090. A certain Meshullam, known as Ibn Shurayq al-Dimashqi, i.e. "the Damascene," dedicates his house to the Great Synagogue of Damascus, to which it is adjacent. The document is a draft written in the hand of Avraham b. Natan, one of the prominent members of the courts in Fustat and Cairo; it is unsigned, and as the name of the donor's father was not yet known, a space was left so it could be inserted later. (Information from Gil, Documents, pp. 214-215 #33)
description_he: מסמך משפטי. שטר הקדשת בית בדמשק לבית הכנסת הגדול, בערך 1090. מישולם הנקרא אבן שוריק הדמשקאי מקדיש את ביתו הסמוך לבית הכנסת. הטיוטה בכתב ידו של אברהם בן נתן.
